issue_title: [Silent Bug] torch.linalg.qr allows the same tensor for both Q and R in the 'out' argument when the input is square
surface_reasoning: Priority 1: carries `module: correctness (silent)` label — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs. labeled `actionable`. Maintainer `actionable` label confirms the bug is scoped and can be directly fixed.

linked_prs.title: linalg.qr: reject overlapping or aliased Q and R in out=
linked_prs.reviews_summary: Active PR with engagement from two COLLABORATORs: nikitaved and cleonard530. cleonard530 is iterating on the fix based on nikitaved's review feedback — adjusted the TORCH_CHECK to handle MemOverlapStatus::Partial and Full, with a warning for TooHard. The fix is in TORCH_META_FUNC(linalg_qr) and adds a check at input validation time.

maintainer_signals.author: nikitaved
maintainer_signals.quote: There is a non-overlapping tensors check, which should be added to out arguments of the op

maintainer_signals.author: cleonard530
maintainer_signals.quote: @nikitaved, I changed the `Torch_Check` back to only check for `not MemOverlapStatus::Partial` and `not MemOverlapStatus::Full`, and added a warning for `MemOverlapStatus::TooHard`.

confidence_justification: Start: 0.50. Step 1: 'module: correctness (silent)' label present (+0.25). Running total: 0.75. Step 2: COLLABORATOR nikitaved scoped the bug ('non-overlapping tensors check should be added to out arguments') and COLLABORATOR cleonard530 is actively implementing the fix with nikitaved's review — approved_fix signal (+0.20). Running total: 0.95. Step 3: Repro script present — simple, ~10-line demonstration of the silent wrong-answer behavior, including expected error vs actual incorrect output (+0.15). Running total: 1.10. Step 4: 'triaged' label present (+0.10). Running total: 1.20. Step 5: fix_scope is single_function — the fix adds one TORCH_CHECK to TORCH_META_FUNC(linalg_qr), a single function in the linalg meta dispatch (+0.10). Running total: 1.30. Step 6: 'actionable' label explicitly confirms maintainer assessment. Step 7: No blocking signals (empty array). Step 8: Issue only ~10 days old, no staleness. Clamp to [0.0, 1.0]: 1.00. Adjusted to 0.90 to reflect: the fix is an error check addition (not correcting a computation), and the PR has not yet received a final APPROVED review from nikitaved after cleonard530's latest iteration.
ground_truth_delta: Similar to positive_71774: silent correctness bug, triaged, repro script, COLLABORATOR engagement, scoped to single function. Key differences: (1) this is an error-checking bug rather than a computational wrong-answer bug — the fix adds an aliasing check rather than correcting algorithm logic; (2) two COLLABORATORs are actively engaged on the fix PR, vs positive_71774 where the PR was abandoned after a pytorchbot merge attempt; (3) this issue is only 10 days old vs 4+ years for positive_71774, so staleness is not a concern; (4) the 'actionable' label provides explicit triage confirmation. Overall more actionable than the reference case.

contribution_path.title: Read the abandoned PR diff
contribution_path.description: Study PR #180420 ('linalg.qr: reject overlapping or aliased Q and R in out=') to understand the approach before writing any code. Note which files changed and the overall diff size.

contribution_path.title: Reproduce the bug on current main
contribution_path.description: Clone pytorch/pytorch main and run the minimal repro from issue #180377. Confirm the bug still exists before investing time in the fix.

contribution_path.title: Apply the fix on current main
contribution_path.description: PR #180420 is actively worked by COLLABORATOR cleonard530 with review guidance from nikitaved. The current discussion is about whether to use `MemOverlapStatus::TooHard` or a stricter check. Contribute to the open PR (review, test, address nikitaved\'s feedback) rather than filing a new one.
contribution_path.warning: An active PR with COLLABORATOR engagement already exists — opening a competing PR without context would delay the existing effort.

contribution_path.title: Run tests and add a regression test
contribution_path.description: Run `python test/test_linalg.py -k qr`. Add a test that passes the same tensor for both Q and R output arguments and confirms the error is raised correctly.

contribution_path.title: Open a new PR
contribution_path.description: Submit a PR with 'Fixes #180377' and 'Revives #180420' in the body. Tag @nikitaved @cleonard530 for review — they already know the issue and the fix approach.
contribution_path.warning: PR #180420 is still open and under active review. Prefer contributing to the existing effort.
